---
title: "High-Dimensional Statistics: Inference in the p > n Regime"
subtitle: "Track A — Biostatistics"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# High-Dimensional Statistics: Inference in the p > n Regime

**Track**: Track A — Biostatistics

When the number of variables exceeds the number of observations — genomics, imaging, electronic health records — classical statistics breaks down. This course covers the theory and methods for valid inference in high dimensions.

## Table of Contents

1. The High-Dimensional Challenge
2. LASSO: Theory and Oracle Inequalities
3. Beyond LASSO: Structured Sparsity
4. Post-Selection Inference
5. Multiple Testing and False Discovery Rate
6. Random Matrix Theory for Statistics
7. Sparse PCA and Dimension Reduction
8. High-Dimensional Bayesian Methods

---

## Chapter 1: The High-Dimensional Challenge

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: LASSO: Theory and Oracle Inequalities

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Beyond LASSO: Structured Sparsity

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Post-Selection Inference

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Multiple Testing and False Discovery Rate

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Random Matrix Theory for Statistics

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Sparse PCA and Dimension Reduction

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: High-Dimensional Bayesian Methods

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
